lesson.claim: The embodied sim-to-real preflight is a replayable local differential-drive proof packet: it declares robot units, command logs, predicted and observed traces, tolerances, safety envelope, latency bound, emits EMBODIED_SIM2REAL_FIXTURE_MATCH, rejects five negative controls, and keeps real robot safety, medical robotics, foundation-model, sim-to-real-at-scale, and BuildLang/buildc-native claims out of scope.
lesson.scope: Project Telos twenty-ninth-wave prooflesson for proof-carrying embodied robotics claims

scaffold.prompt: Restate, in your own words, exactly what this claim asserts and nothing more: "The embodied sim-to-real preflight is a replayable local differential-drive proof packet: it declares robot units, command logs, predicted and observed traces, tolerances, safety envelope, latency bound, emits EMBODIED_SIM2REAL_FIXTURE_MATCH, rejects five negative controls, and keeps real robot safety, medical robotics, foundation-model, sim-to-real-at-scale, and BuildLang/buildc-native claims out of scope." (scope: Project Telos twenty-ninth-wave prooflesson for proof-carrying embodied robotics claims).

Judging by their refs alone (demo/research/embodied-sim2real-source-receipts.json, demo/embodied-sim2real-proof-packet.mjs, demo/embodied-sim2real-proof-packet.test.mjs, docs/outreach/receipts/twenty-ninth-wave/embodied-sim2real-proof-packet-2026-07-02.json, docs/outreach/receipts/twenty-ninth-wave-embodied-sim2real-run-2026-07-02.json, docs/outreach/receipts/twenty-ninth-wave-embodied-sim2real-report-2026-07-02.md), predict what each recorded source could contribute as evidence for or against the claim.
The packet's overall verdict is MATCH. Before reading anything else, derive what a MATCH verdict must mean about the relationship between the claim and the recorded checks.
List the checks you would run to reach your own verdict on this claim, then compare your list against what the packet's verdict implies was actually checked.

retrievalQuestions.question: What evidence would falsify this claim: "The embodied sim-to-real preflight is a replayable local differential-drive proof packet: it declares robot units, command logs, predicted and observed traces, tolerances, safety envelope, latency bound, emits EMBODIED_SIM2REAL_FIXTURE_MATCH, rejects five negative controls, and keeps real robot safety, medical robotics, foundation-model, sim-to-real-at-scale, and BuildLang/buildc-native claims out of scope."?
Which recorded source would you re-check first, and what does its content hash let you prove about it?
What does an overall verdict of MATCH entitle you to claim, and what does it not?
The packet's scope is "Project Telos twenty-ninth-wave prooflesson for proof-carrying embodied robotics claims". Give one claim that would exceed that scope.

lesson.boundary: Lesson scaffold only -- prompts for the operator to derive the reasoning; never an answer dump, never a graded-assessment answer.
